questions.q: Paragraph: The complex slope of a line passing through two points represented by complex numbers $z_1$ and $z_2$ is defined by $\frac{z_2-z_1}{z_2-z_1}$ and we shall denote by $\omega$. If $z_0$ is complex number and $c$ is a real number, then $\bar{z}_0 z+z_0 \bar{z}+c=0$ represents a straight line. Its complex slope is $-\frac{z_0}{\bar{z}_0}$. Now consider two lines $\alpha \bar{z}+\bar{\alpha} z+i \beta=0$ ...(i) and $a \bar{z}+\bar{a} z+b=0$ ...(ii) where $\alpha, \beta$ and $\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}$ are complex constants and let their complex slopes be denoted by $\omega_1$ and $\omega_2$ respectivelyQuestion: If line (i) makes an angle of $45^{\circ}$ with real axis, then $(1+i)\left(-\frac{2 \alpha}{\bar{\alpha}}\right)$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & -\frac{\alpha}{\alpha}=\mathrm{e}^{ \pm \frac{\pi}{2}}= \pm \mathrm{i} \\ & \therefore(1+\mathrm{i})\left(-\frac{2 \theta}{\alpha}\right)= \pm 2(-1+\mathrm{i})\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: The complex slope of a line passing through two points represented by complex numbers $z_1$ and $z_2$ is defined by $\frac{z_2-z_1}{z_2-z_1}$ and we shall denote by $\omega$. If $z_0$ is complex number and $c$ is a real number, then $\bar{z}_0 z+z_0 \bar{z}+c=0$ represents a straight line. Its complex slope is $-\frac{z_0}{\bar{z}_0}$. Now consider two lines $\alpha \bar{z}+\bar{\alpha} z+i \beta=0$ ...(i) and $a \bar{z}+\bar{a} z+b=0$ ...(ii) where $\alpha, \beta$ and $\mathrm{a}, \mathrm{b}$ are complex constants and let their complex slopes be denoted by $\omega_1$ and $\omega_2$ respectivelyQuestion: If the lines are inclined at an angle of $120^{\circ}$ to each other, then
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \omega_1=\omega_2 \mathrm{e}^{i \frac{4 \pi}{3}} \\ & \Rightarrow \omega_1^3=\omega_2^3\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \omega_1^3 \bar{\omega}_1^2 \bar{\omega}_2^2=\omega_2^3 \bar{\omega}_1^2 \bar{\omega}_2^2 \\ & \Rightarrow \omega_2 \bar{\omega}_1^2=\omega_1 \bar{\omega}_2^2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: In argand plane consider $\mathrm{A}\left(\mathrm{z}_1\right), \mathrm{B}\left(\mathrm{z}_2\right)$ and $\mathrm{C}\left(\mathrm{z}_3\right)$ as distinct complex numbers lying on the curve $\left|\mathrm{z}^2\right|=\sqrt{3}$.Question: If $\triangle A B C$ is equilateral and $P\left(z_p\right)$ be any point such that $\left|z_i\right|=\left|z_p\right|$, then the values of $\left|z_p-z_1\right|^2+\left|z_p-z_2\right|^2+\left|z_p-z_3\right|^2$ is
questions.solution:
Let $z_{\infty}, z_p$ be roots of the equation $z_1 z^2+z_2 z+z_3=0$ such that $\left|z_d\right|=1$ then
$\left(z_\alpha+z_\beta\right)^{-}=z_\alpha z_\beta$
$\left(\frac{-z_2}{z_1}\right)^{-}=\frac{z_3}{z_1} \Rightarrow z_2^2=z_1 z_3$
Hence $z_1, z_2, z_3$ are in G.P.
Since $z_1^2+z_2^2+z_3^2=z_1 z_2+z_2 z_3+z_3 z_1$
$\Rightarrow \triangle A B C$ is equilateral.
Also $\mathrm{PA}^2+\mathrm{PB}^2+\mathrm{PC}^2=3\left|z_1\right|^2-\left(1+\omega+\omega^2\right) z_1^{\prime} \bar{z}-\left(1+\omega+\omega^2\right) z_1 z+\left|z_1\right|^2+\left|z_1\right|^2+\left|z_1\right|^2=6 r^2=18$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: Paragraph: In argand plane consider $\mathrm{A}\left(\mathrm{z}_1\right), \mathrm{B}\left(\mathrm{z}_2\right)$ and $\mathrm{C}\left(\mathrm{z}_3\right)$ as distinct complex numbers lying on the curve $\left|\mathrm{z}^2\right|=\sqrt{3}$.Question: If $z_1 z^2+z_2 z+z_3=0$ and $z_2 z^2+z_3 z+z_1=0$ each have a common root having modulus unity then $\triangle \mathrm{ABC}$ is
questions.solution:
Let $z_{\infty}, z_p$ be roots of the equation $z_1 z^2+z_2 z+z_3=0$ such that $\left|z_d\right|=1$ then
$\left(z_\alpha+z_\beta\right)^{-}=z_\alpha z_\beta$
$\left(\frac{-z_2}{z_1}\right)^{-}=\frac{z_3}{z_1} \Rightarrow z_2^2=z_1 z_3$
Hence $z_1, z_2, z_3$ are in G.P.
Since $z_1^2+z_2^2+z_3^2=z_1 z_2+z_2 z_3+z_3 z_1$
$\Rightarrow \triangle A B C$ is equilateral.
Also $\mathrm{PA}^2+\mathrm{PB}^2+\mathrm{PC}^2=3\left|z_1\right|^2-\left(1+\omega+\omega^2\right) z_1^{\prime} \bar{z}-\left(1+\omega+\omega^2\right) z_1 z+\left|z_1\right|^2+\left|z_1\right|^2+\left|z_1\right|^2=6 r^2=18$.

questions.q: Paragraph:Consider the equation $a z+\dot{b} \bar{z}+c=0$, where $a, b, c \in \mathbf{C}$ (set of complex number)Question: If $|\mathrm{a}|=|\mathrm{b}| \neq 0$ and $\overline{\mathrm{a}} \mathrm{c}=\mathrm{b} \overline{\mathrm{c}}$, then $\mathrm{az}+\mathrm{b} \overline{\mathrm{z}}+\mathrm{c}=0$ represents
questions.solution:
$a z+b \bar{z}+c=0.......(1)$ 
$\Rightarrow \overline{\mathrm{a}} \overline{\mathrm{z}}+\overline{\mathrm{b}} \mathrm{z}+\overline{\mathrm{c}}=0.......(2)$
Eliminating $\bar{z}$ from (1) and (2)
$\left(|b|^2-|a|^2\right) z=c \bar{a}-b \bar{c}$
Adding (1) and (2) $(\overline{\mathrm{a}}+\mathrm{b}) \overline{\mathrm{z}}+(\mathrm{a}+\overline{\mathrm{b}}) \mathrm{z}+(\mathrm{c}+\overline{\mathrm{c}})=0$
This is of form $A \bar{z}+\bar{A} z+B=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph:Consider the equation $a z+\dot{b} \bar{z}+c=0$, where $a, b, c \in \mathbf{C}$ (set of complex number)Question:If $|a|=|b|$ and $\bar{a} c \neq b \bar{c}$, then $z$ has
questions.solution:
$a z+b \bar{z}+c=0.......(1)$ 
$\Rightarrow \overline{\mathrm{a}} \overline{\mathrm{z}}+\overline{\mathrm{b}} \mathrm{z}+\overline{\mathrm{c}}=0.......(2)$
Eliminating $\bar{z}$ from (1) and (2)
$\left(|b|^2-|a|^2\right) z=c \bar{a}-b \bar{c}$
Adding (1) and (2) $(\overline{\mathrm{a}}+\mathrm{b}) \overline{\mathrm{z}}+(\mathrm{a}+\overline{\mathrm{b}}) \mathrm{z}+(\mathrm{c}+\overline{\mathrm{c}})=0$
This is of form $A \bar{z}+\bar{A} z+B=0$

If $z_0$ is complex number and $c$ is a real number, then $\bar{z}_0 z+z_0 \bar{z}+c=0$ represents a straight line. Its complex slope is $-\frac{z_0}{\bar{z}_0}$. Since ip is real
$\therefore \beta$ is purely imaginary

questions.solution:
Let $z_{\infty}, z_p$ be roots of the equation $z_1 z^2+z_2 z+z_3=0$ such that $\left|z_d\right|=1$ then
$\left(z_\alpha+z_\beta\right)^{-}=z_\alpha z_\beta$
$\left(\frac{-z_2}{z_1}\right)^{-}=\frac{z_3}{z_1} \Rightarrow z_2^2=z_1 z_3$

questions.q:
Paragraph: For non-zero complex numbers $z_1$ and $z_2$ define the dot product and the cross product of $\mathrm{z}_1$ and $\mathrm{z}_2$ as follows Dot product $z_1 \cdot z_2=\left|z_1\right|\left|z_2\right| \cos \theta=x_1 x_2+y_1 y_2=\operatorname{Re}\left(\bar{z}_1 z_2\right)$ Cross product $z_1 \times z_2=\left|z_1\right|\left|z_2\right| \sin \theta=x_1 y_2-x_2 y_1=i m\left(\bar{z}_1 z_2\right)$
Question: $z_1 z_2+i\left(z_1 \times z_2\right)$ equals
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & z_1 \cdot z_2+i\left(z_1 \times z_2\right)=\left|z_1\right|\left|z_2\right| \cos \theta+i\left|z_1\right|\left|z_2\right| \sin \theta \\ & =\left|z_1\right|\left|z_2\right| e^{\prime \theta} \text { where } \theta=\beta-\alpha \\ & =\left(\left|z_1\right| e^{-i \alpha}\right)\left(\left|z_2\right| e^\phi\right)=z_2 \overline{z_1}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: For non-zero complex numbers $z_1$ and $z_2$ define the dot product and the cross product of $\mathrm{z}_1$ and $\mathrm{z}_2$ as follows Dot product $z_1 \cdot z_2=\left|z_1\right|\left|z_2\right| \cos \theta=x_1 x_2+y_1 y_2=\operatorname{Re}\left(\bar{z}_1 z_2\right)$ Cross product $z_1 \times z_2=\left|z_1\right|\left|z_2\right| \sin \theta=x_1 y_2-x_2 y_1=i m\left(\bar{z}_1 z_2\right)$
Question:$z_1 \cdot z_2==0$ is equivalent to say
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & z_1 \cdot z_2=\left|z_1\right|\left|z_2\right| \cos \theta=0 \\ & \operatorname{Re}\left(\overline{z_1} z_2\right)=0 \\ & \bar{z}_1 z_2=i k \\ & \frac{z_2}{z_1}\left(\bar{z} \overline{z_1}\right)=i k \\ & \frac{z_2}{z_1}=i k\end{aligned}$
Also even if $z_1=0$ or $z_2=0$
$z_1 \cdot z_2=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: For non-zero complex numbers $z_1$ and $z_2$ define the dot product and the cross product of $\mathrm{z}_1$ and $\mathrm{z}_2$ as follows Dot product $z_1 \cdot z_2=\left|z_1\right|\left|z_2\right| \cos \theta=x_1 x_2+y_1 y_2=\operatorname{Re}\left(\bar{z}_1 z_2\right)$ Cross product $z_1 \times z_2=\left|z_1\right|\left|z_2\right| \sin \theta=x_1 y_2-x_2 y_1=i m\left(\bar{z}_1 z_2\right)$
Question: OP coincides with $O Q$ if and only if
questions.solution: $O P$ coincides with $O Q$ if $Q=0, z_1 \times z_2=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $A$ be a variable point on locus of feet of perpendicular drawn from focus upon any tangent to the curve $|z-2|+|z+2|=6$ and $B$ be a variable point on $(1-i) z+(1+i) \bar{z}=10 \sqrt{2}$, thenQuestion: Minimum value of $A B$ is:
questions.solution:
(I) $|z-2|+|z+2|=6$ represents an ellipse with major axis 6 and focus $(2,0)$ and $(-2,0)$
$\therefore \quad A$ lies on its auxilliary circle $i$. e., $x^2+y^2=9$ and $(1-i) z+(1+i) \bar{z}=10 \sqrt{2} \quad \Rightarrow \quad(z+\bar{z})-i(z-\bar{z})=10 \sqrt{2}$ $\Rightarrow \quad 2 x-i(2 i y)=10 \sqrt{2} \Rightarrow x+y=5 \sqrt{2}$ $\therefore \quad B$ lies on straight line $x+y=5 \sqrt{2}$ $\therefore \quad A B_{\min }=$ perpendicular distance $O B-$ radius $O A=5-3=2$ (II) If a variable circle touches line and circle, then locus will be the Parabola with focus at centre $(0,0)$ of given circle and directrix at a line parallel to given line at a distance of 3 units, equal to radius of circle $\therefore \quad$ Distance between focus and directrix $=5+3=8$ $\therefore \quad$ Latus rectum $=16$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $A$ be a variable point on locus of feet of perpendicular drawn from focus upon any tangent to the curve $|z-2|+|z+2|=6$ and $B$ be a variable point on $(1-i) z+(1+i) \bar{z}=10 \sqrt{2}$, thenQuestion: If a variable circle touches both the loci on which $A$ and $B$ externally lie then latus rectum of locus of centre of variable circle is:
questions.solution:
(I) $|z-2|+|z+2|=6$ represents an ellipse with major axis 6 and focus $(2,0)$ and $(-2,0)$
$\therefore \quad A$ lies on its auxilliary circle $i$. e., $x^2+y^2=9$ and $(1-i) z+(1+i) \bar{z}=10 \sqrt{2} \quad \Rightarrow \quad(z+\bar{z})-i(z-\bar{z})=10 \sqrt{2}$ $\Rightarrow \quad 2 x-i(2 i y)=10 \sqrt{2} \Rightarrow x+y=5 \sqrt{2}$ $\therefore \quad B$ lies on straight line $x+y=5 \sqrt{2}$ $\therefore \quad A B_{\min }=$ perpendicular distance $O B-$ radius $O A=5-3=2$ (II) If a variable circle touches line and circle, then locus will be the Parabola with focus at centre $(0,0)$ of given circle and directrix at a line parallel to given line at a distance of 3 units, equal to radius of circle $\therefore \quad$ Distance between focus and directrix $=5+3=8$ $\therefore \quad$ Latus rectum $=16$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph Roots of $z^n-1=0$ are $1, \alpha, \alpha^2, \alpha^3, \ldots \ldots . . \alpha^{n-1}$ where $\alpha=\cos \frac{2 \pi}{n}+i \sin \frac{2 \pi}{n}$ so $z^n-1=(z-1)(z-\alpha)$ $\qquad$ $\left(\mathrm{z}-\alpha^{\mathrm{n}-1}\right)$ On the basis of above information, answer the following questions :Question $\sum_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{\mathrm{n}-1} \ln \sin \frac{\mathrm{r} \pi}{\mathrm{n}}$ is equal to $\left(\mathrm{n} \in \mathrm{I}^{+}\right)$
questions.solution:
$\because \quad z^{\mathrm{n}}-1=(\mathrm{z}-1)(\mathrm{z}-\alpha)\left(\mathrm{z}-\alpha^2\right) \ldots \ldots .\left(\mathrm{z}-\alpha^{\mathrm{n}-1}\right)$
$\Rightarrow \frac{z^n-1}{z-1}=(z-\alpha)\left(z-\alpha^2\right) \ldots \ldots\left(z-\alpha^{n-1}\right)$
$\Rightarrow \lim _{z \rightarrow 1} \frac{z^n-1}{z-1}=\lim _{z \rightarrow 1}(z-\alpha)\left(z-\alpha^2\right) \ldots \ldots .\left(z-\alpha^{n-1}\right)$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{n}=\prod_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{\mathrm{n}-1}\left(1-\alpha^{\mathrm{r}}\right)$
$=\prod_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{\mathrm{n}-1}\left(1-\alpha^{\frac{2 \pi \mathrm{ri}}{\mathrm{n}}}\right)$
$=\prod_{r=1}^{n-1}\left\{-e^{\frac{\pi \mathrm{i}}{\mathrm{n}}}\left(e^{\frac{\pi \mathrm{i}}{n}}-e^{-\frac{\pi \mathrm{i}}{n}}\right)\right\}=\prod_{r=1}^{\mathrm{n}-1}-e^{\frac{\pi \mathrm{i}}{\mathrm{n}}} 2 \mathrm{i} \sin \left(\frac{\mathrm{r} \pi}{\mathrm{n}}\right)$
$\Rightarrow|n|=\prod_{r=1}^{\mathrm{n}-1} 2 \sin \left(\frac{\mathrm{r} \pi}{\mathrm{n}}\right)$
$\Rightarrow|n|=2^{\mathrm{n}-1} \prod_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{\mathrm{n}-1} \sin \left(\frac{\mathrm{r} \pi}{\mathrm{n}}\right)$
$\Rightarrow \prod_{\mathrm{r}=1}^{\mathrm{n}-1} \sin \left(\frac{\mathrm{r} \pi}{\mathrm{n}}\right)=\frac{\mathrm{n}}{2^{\mathrm{n}-1}} \quad(\because \mathrm{n}\gt1)$
So $\sum_{r=1}^{n-1} \ell n \sin \frac{r \pi}{n}=\ell n\left(\prod_{r=1}^{n-1} \sin \frac{r \pi}{n}\right)$
$=\ell \mathrm{n} \frac{\mathrm{n}}{2^{\mathrm{n}-1}}=\ell \mathrm{nn}-(\mathrm{n}-1) \ell \mathrm{n} 2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph Roots of $z^n-1=0$ are $1, \alpha, \alpha^2, \alpha^3, \ldots \ldots . . \alpha^{n-1}$ where $\alpha=\cos \frac{2 \pi}{n}+i \sin \frac{2 \pi}{n}$ so $z^n-1=(z-1)(z-\alpha)$ $\qquad$ $\left(\mathrm{z}-\alpha^{\mathrm{n}-1}\right)$ On the basis of above information, answer the following questions :Question If $\mathrm{n}=7$, then the equation whose roots are $\alpha+\alpha^2+\alpha^4$ and $\alpha^3+\alpha^5+\alpha^6$ is -
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{n}=7, \alpha^7=1 \text { and } 1+\alpha+\alpha^2+\ldots \ldots . . \alpha^6=0$
Sum of the roots $=\alpha+\alpha^2+\alpha^4+\alpha^3+\alpha^5+\alpha^6=-1$
Product of the roots $=\left(\alpha+\alpha^2+\alpha^4\right)\left(\alpha^3+\alpha^5+\alpha^6\right)$
$=\alpha^4+\alpha^6+\alpha^7+\alpha^5+\alpha^7+\alpha^8+\alpha^7+\alpha^9+\alpha^{10}$
$=2+\left(1+\alpha+\alpha^2+\alpha^3+\alpha^4+\alpha^5+\alpha^6\right)=2$
So the required equation is
$x^2-(-1) x+2=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad x^2+x+2=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph Roots of $z^n-1=0$ are $1, \alpha, \alpha^2, \alpha^3, \ldots \ldots . . \alpha^{n-1}$ where $\alpha=\cos \frac{2 \pi}{n}+i \sin \frac{2 \pi}{n}$ so $z^n-1=(z-1)(z-\alpha)$ $\qquad$ $\left(\mathrm{z}-\alpha^{\mathrm{n}-1}\right)$ On the basis of above information, answer the following questions :Question If $\mathrm{n}=5$, then $(1+\alpha)\left(1+\alpha^2\right)\left(1+\alpha^3\right)\left(1+\alpha^4\right)$ is equal to -
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{n}=5, \alpha^5=1 \\ & \text { and } 1+\alpha+\alpha^2+\alpha^3+\alpha^4=0 \\ & (1+\alpha)\left(1+\alpha^2\right)\left(1+\alpha^3\right)\left(1+\alpha^4\right) \\ & =\left(1+\alpha+\alpha^2+\alpha^3\right)\left(1+\alpha^3+\alpha^4+\alpha^7\right) \\ & =\left(-\alpha^4\right)\left(1+\alpha^2+\alpha^3+\alpha^4\right) \\ & =\left(-\alpha^4\right)(-\alpha)=\alpha^5=1\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph Consider a circle centred at origin O and passing through points $\mathrm{A}\left(\mathrm{z}_1\right), \mathrm{B}\left(\mathrm{z}_2\right)$ and $\mathrm{C}\left(\mathrm{z}_3\right)$. The tangents to the circle at $\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}$ and C intersect at $\mathrm{D}, \mathrm{E}$ and $\mathrm{F} ; \mathrm{D}, \mathrm{E}, \mathrm{F}$ are opposite to $\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}, \mathrm{C}$ respectively. Also $\angle \mathrm{AOB}=\angle \mathrm{BOC}=\theta$, where $\theta \in\left(\frac{\pi}{2}, \pi\right)$ satisfying the equation $\sqrt{17 \sec ^2 x+16\left(\frac{1}{2} \tan x \sec x-1\right)}=-2 \tan x(1+4 \sin x)$ On the basis of above information, answer the following :Question Which of the following is not the equation of tangent to the circle at A ( z represents any variable point on the tangent at A) -
questions.solution: $\mathrm{OA} \perp \mathrm{AP} \Rightarrow \arg \left(\mathrm{z}-\mathrm{z}_1\right)-\arg \left(\mathrm{z}_1\right)= \pm \frac{\pi}{2}$ or $\arg \left(\frac{z-z_1}{z_1}\right)= \pm \frac{\pi}{2}$ $\Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{z}-\mathrm{z}_1}{\mathrm{z}_1}$ is purely imaginary. or $\frac{z-z_1}{z_1}=\lambda i \Rightarrow z=z_1(1+\lambda i), \lambda \in R_0$ Also complex slope of AP + complex slope of $\mathrm{OA}=0$ $\Rightarrow \frac{z-z_1}{\bar{z}-\overline{z_1}}+\frac{z_1}{z_1}=0$ or $\frac{z-z_1}{z_1}+\frac{\bar{z}-\overline{z_1}}{\overline{z_1}}=0$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph Consider a circle centred at origin O and passing through points $\mathrm{A}\left(\mathrm{z}_1\right), \mathrm{B}\left(\mathrm{z}_2\right)$ and $\mathrm{C}\left(\mathrm{z}_3\right)$. The tangents to the circle at $\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}$ and C intersect at $\mathrm{D}, \mathrm{E}$ and $\mathrm{F} ; \mathrm{D}, \mathrm{E}, \mathrm{F}$ are opposite to $\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}, \mathrm{C}$ respectively. Also $\angle \mathrm{AOB}=\angle \mathrm{BOC}=\theta$, where $\theta \in\left(\frac{\pi}{2}, \pi\right)$ satisfying the equation $\sqrt{17 \sec ^2 x+16\left(\frac{1}{2} \tan x \sec x-1\right)}=-2 \tan x(1+4 \sin x)$ On the basis of above information, answer the following :Question The complex representation of intersection point F is given by -
questions.solution:
Let $\left|z_1\right|=\left|z_2\right|=\left|z_3\right|=a$
If $z$ be the complex representation of $F$, then
$\frac{z-z_1}{z_1}+\frac{\bar{z}-\bar{z}_1}{\bar{z}_1}=0$
i.e., $\bar{z}_1 z+\bar{z} z_1=2 a^2...(i)$
similarly, $\bar{z}_2 z+\bar{z} z_2=2 a^2$....(ii)
From (i) and (ii), we have
$\left(\bar{z}_1 z_2-\bar{z}_2 z_1\right) z=2 a^2\left(z_2-z_1\right)$
$\mathrm{a}^2\left(\frac{\mathrm{z}_2}{\mathrm{z}_1}-\frac{\mathrm{z}_1}{\mathrm{z}_2}\right) \mathrm{z}=2 \mathrm{a}^2\left(\mathrm{z}_2-\mathrm{z}_1\right)$ or $\mathrm{z}=\frac{2 \mathrm{z}_1 \mathrm{z}_2}{z_1+\mathrm{z}_2}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph Consider a circle centred at origin O and passing through points $\mathrm{A}\left(\mathrm{z}_1\right), \mathrm{B}\left(\mathrm{z}_2\right)$ and $\mathrm{C}\left(\mathrm{z}_3\right)$. The tangents to the circle at $\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}$ and C intersect at $\mathrm{D}, \mathrm{E}$ and $\mathrm{F} ; \mathrm{D}, \mathrm{E}, \mathrm{F}$ are opposite to $\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}, \mathrm{C}$ respectively. Also $\angle \mathrm{AOB}=\angle \mathrm{BOC}=\theta$, where $\theta \in\left(\frac{\pi}{2}, \pi\right)$ satisfying the equation $\sqrt{17 \sec ^2 x+16\left(\frac{1}{2} \tan x \sec x-1\right)}=-2 \tan x(1+4 \sin x)$ On the basis of above information, answer the following :Question $\mathrm{z}_1, \mathrm{z}_2, \mathrm{z}_3$ are in G.P. with common ratio r , then $[\mathrm{i} \ell \mathrm{nr}]$ is equal to (where [.] denotes greatest integer function) -
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{z}_2=\mathrm{z}_1 \cdot e^{\frac{\mathrm{i} 5 \pi}{6}}$ and $\mathrm{z}_3=\mathrm{z}_2 \cdot e^{\frac{\mathrm{i} 5 \pi}{6}}$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{z}_1, \mathrm{z}_2, \mathrm{z}_3$ are in G.P. with common ratio $e^{\frac{\mathrm{i} 5 \pi}{6}}$.
$\therefore \mathrm{i} \ell \mathrm{nr}=-\frac{5 \pi}{6}$
$\Rightarrow \quad[\mathrm{i} \ell \mathrm{nr}]=-3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph Let the origin $O$ of the argand plane represents the home town of a person. Suppose the man travels $\sqrt{2}$ units in north-east direction to reach city A. Then he travels 1 unit in east direction to reach town $B$. From town $B$ he starts to move along a circular path, in anticlockwise direction with centre $O$ and radius OB , to reach to city C such that $\angle \mathrm{BOC}$ is right angle. Finally he returns home by the shortest path from city $C$. Let $z_1, z_2$ and $z_3$ be the complex numbers corresponding to the points $A, B$ and $C$ respectively. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question Let $z_p \& z_q$ be the two complex numbers satisfying the equations $\left|z-z_2\right|=1 \&\left|z-z_3\right|=1$ respectively, then maximum value of $\left|\mathrm{z}_{\mathrm{p}}-\mathrm{z}_{\mathrm{q}}\right|$ is -
questions.solution: From figure maximum value of $\left|z_p-z_q\right|=$ $\sqrt{9+1}+2=2+\sqrt{10}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph Let the origin $O$ of the argand plane represents the home town of a person. Suppose the man travels $\sqrt{2}$ units in north-east direction to reach city A. Then he travels 1 unit in east direction to reach town $B$. From town $B$ he starts to move along a circular path, in anticlockwise direction with centre $O$ and radius OB , to reach to city C such that $\angle \mathrm{BOC}$ is right angle. Finally he returns home by the shortest path from city $C$. Let $z_1, z_2$ and $z_3$ be the complex numbers corresponding to the points $A, B$ and $C$ respectively. On the basis of above information answer the following :Question The image of complex number $z_2$ under the locus $\left|z-z_1\right|=\left|z-z_3\right|-$
questions.solution: Solving $\left|z-z_1\right|=\left|z-z_3\right|$, we get $z(-2-i)+\bar{z}(-2+i)=3...(i)$ Let $z_p$ be the image of $z_2$ in (i) $\Rightarrow z_p(-2-i)+\bar{z}_2(-2+i)=3$ $\Rightarrow z_p=\frac{-8}{5}+\frac{14}{5} i$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $\omega$ be the complex number representing the point $M\left(\frac{-1}{2}, \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right)$ and $a, b, c, \alpha, \beta, \gamma$ be non-zero complex numbers such that $$ \begin{aligned} & a+b+c=\alpha \\ & a+b \omega+c \omega^2=\beta \\ & a+b \omega^2+c \omega=\gamma . \end{aligned} $$Question: If $|\alpha|^2+|\beta|^2+|\gamma|^2=\lambda\left(|a|^2+|b|^2+|c|^2\right)$ then $\lambda$ is equal to:
questions.solution:
(i)
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { Given, } \quad a+b+c=\alpha, a+b \omega+c \omega^2=\beta, a+b \omega^2+c \omega=\gamma \\
& |\alpha|^2+|\beta|^2+|\gamma|^2=\alpha \bar{\alpha}+\beta \bar{\beta}+\bar{\gamma} \quad\left[\because \bar{\omega}=\omega^2\right] \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
Now,
$$
\begin{aligned}
\alpha \bar{\alpha} & =(a+b+c)(\bar{a}+\bar{b}+\bar{c}) \quad\left[\bar{\omega}^2=\omega\right] \\
& =|a|^2+|b|^2+|c|^2+a \bar{b}+a \bar{c}+b \bar{a}+b \bar{c}+c \bar{a}+c \bar{b} \\
\beta \bar{\beta} & =\left(a+b \omega+c \omega^2\right)\left(\bar{a}+\bar{b} \omega^2+\bar{c} \omega\right)
\end{aligned}
$$

$$
\begin{aligned}
& =|a|^2+|b|^2+|c|^2+a \bar{b} \omega^2+a \bar{c} \omega+b \bar{a} \omega+b \bar{c} \omega^2+c \bar{a} \omega^2+c \bar{b} \omega \\
\bar{\gamma} & =\left(a+b \omega^2+c \omega\right)\left(\bar{a}+\bar{b} \omega+\bar{c} \omega^2\right) \\
& =|a|^2+|b|^2+|c|^2+a \bar{b} \omega+a \bar{c} \omega^2+b \bar{a} \omega^2+b \bar{c} \omega+c \bar{a} \omega+c \bar{b} \omega^2
\end{aligned}
$$

So,
$$
\begin{array}{rlc}
\alpha \bar{\alpha}+\beta \bar{\beta}+\gamma \bar{\gamma} & =3\left(|a|^2+|b|^2+|c|^2\right)+(a \bar{b}+a \bar{c}+b \bar{a}+b \bar{c}+c \bar{a}+c \bar{b})\left(1+\omega+\omega^2\right) \\
& =3\left(|a|^2+|b|^2+|c|^2\right) & {\left[\because 1+\omega+\omega^2=0\right]}
\end{array}
$$

So, $\quad \lambda=3$
(ii)
$$
\begin{aligned}
& (z+1)\left|\begin{array}{cc}
z+\omega^2 & 1 \\
1 & z+\omega
\end{array}\right|+\omega\left|\begin{array}{cc}
1 & \omega \\
z+\omega & \omega^2
\end{array}\right|+\omega^2\left|\begin{array}{cc}
\omega & z+\omega^2 \\
\omega^2 & 1
\end{array}\right|=0 \\
\text { As } & 1+\omega+\omega^2=0, \quad \omega^4=\omega \\
\Rightarrow & (z+1)\left(z^2+z\left(\omega+\omega^2\right)+\omega^3-1\right)+\omega\left(\omega^2-z \omega-\omega^2\right)+\omega^2\left(\omega-z \omega^2-\omega^4\right)=0 \\
\Rightarrow & (z+1)\left(z^2-z\right)+z\left(-\omega^2-\omega\right)=0 \\
\Rightarrow & z^3-z^2+z^2-z+z=0 \\
\Rightarrow & z^3=0 \\
\Rightarrow & z=0 \text { only } 1 \text { complex number. }
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $\omega$ be the complex number representing the point $M\left(\frac{-1}{2}, \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right)$ and $a, b, c, \alpha, \beta, \gamma$ be non-zero complex numbers such that $$ \begin{aligned} & a+b+c=\alpha \\ & a+b \omega+c \omega^2=\beta \\ & a+b \omega^2+c \omega=\gamma . \end{aligned} $$Question: Number of distinct complex numbers $z$ satisfying the equation: $$ (z+1)\left|\begin{array}{cc} z+\omega^2 & 1 \\ 1 & z+\omega \end{array}\right|+\omega\left|\begin{array}{cc} 1 & \omega \\ z+\omega & \omega^2 \end{array}\right|+\omega^2\left|\begin{array}{cc} \omega & z+\omega^2 \\ \omega^2 & 1 \end{array}\right|=0 $$ is equal to:
questions.solution:
(i)
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { Given, } \quad a+b+c=\alpha, a+b \omega+c \omega^2=\beta, a+b \omega^2+c \omega=\gamma \\
& |\alpha|^2+|\beta|^2+|\gamma|^2=\alpha \bar{\alpha}+\beta \bar{\beta}+\bar{\gamma} \quad\left[\because \bar{\omega}=\omega^2\right] \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
Now,
$$
\begin{aligned}
\alpha \bar{\alpha} & =(a+b+c)(\bar{a}+\bar{b}+\bar{c}) \quad\left[\bar{\omega}^2=\omega\right] \\
& =|a|^2+|b|^2+|c|^2+a \bar{b}+a \bar{c}+b \bar{a}+b \bar{c}+c \bar{a}+c \bar{b} \\
\beta \bar{\beta} & =\left(a+b \omega+c \omega^2\right)\left(\bar{a}+\bar{b} \omega^2+\bar{c} \omega\right)
\end{aligned}
$$

$$
\begin{aligned}
& =|a|^2+|b|^2+|c|^2+a \bar{b} \omega^2+a \bar{c} \omega+b \bar{a} \omega+b \bar{c} \omega^2+c \bar{a} \omega^2+c \bar{b} \omega \\
\bar{\gamma} & =\left(a+b \omega^2+c \omega\right)\left(\bar{a}+\bar{b} \omega+\bar{c} \omega^2\right) \\
& =|a|^2+|b|^2+|c|^2+a \bar{b} \omega+a \bar{c} \omega^2+b \bar{a} \omega^2+b \bar{c} \omega+c \bar{a} \omega+c \bar{b} \omega^2
\end{aligned}
$$

So,
$$
\begin{array}{rlc}
\alpha \bar{\alpha}+\beta \bar{\beta}+\gamma \bar{\gamma} & =3\left(|a|^2+|b|^2+|c|^2\right)+(a \bar{b}+a \bar{c}+b \bar{a}+b \bar{c}+c \bar{a}+c \bar{b})\left(1+\omega+\omega^2\right) \\
& =3\left(|a|^2+|b|^2+|c|^2\right) & {\left[\because 1+\omega+\omega^2=0\right]}
\end{array}
$$

So, $\quad \lambda=3$
(ii)
$$
\begin{aligned}
& (z+1)\left|\begin{array}{cc}
z+\omega^2 & 1 \\
1 & z+\omega
\end{array}\right|+\omega\left|\begin{array}{cc}
1 & \omega \\
z+\omega & \omega^2
\end{array}\right|+\omega^2\left|\begin{array}{cc}
\omega & z+\omega^2 \\
\omega^2 & 1
\end{array}\right|=0 \\
\text { As } & 1+\omega+\omega^2=0, \quad \omega^4=\omega \\
\Rightarrow & (z+1)\left(z^2+z\left(\omega+\omega^2\right)+\omega^3-1\right)+\omega\left(\omega^2-z \omega-\omega^2\right)+\omega^2\left(\omega-z \omega^2-\omega^4\right)=0 \\
\Rightarrow & (z+1)\left(z^2-z\right)+z\left(-\omega^2-\omega\right)=0 \\
\Rightarrow & z^3-z^2+z^2-z+z=0 \\
\Rightarrow & z^3=0 \\
\Rightarrow & z=0 \text { only } 1 \text { complex number. }
\end{aligned}
$$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the two complex numbers $z$ and $w$ such that $w=\frac{Z-1}{Z+2}=a+b i$, where $a, b \in R$.Question: If $z=\operatorname{CiS} \theta$ then, which of the following does hold good?
questions.solution:
(i) Consider $z=\operatorname{CiS} \theta$ and $a+i b=\frac{z-1}{z+2}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow a+i b=\frac{\operatorname{Cis} \theta-1}{\operatorname{Cis} \theta+2}=\frac{(\cos \theta-1)+i \sin \theta}{(\cos \theta+2)+i \sin \theta}=\frac{((\cos \theta-1)+i \sin \theta)((\cos \theta+2)-i \sin \theta)}{(\cos \theta+2)^2+\sin ^2 \theta} \\
& =\frac{\left((\cos \theta-1)(\cos \theta+2)+\sin ^2 \theta\right)+i\left((\cos \theta+2) \sin ^\theta \theta-(\cos \theta-1) \sin \theta\right)}{\cos ^2 \theta+4 \cos \theta+4+\sin ^2 \theta} \\
& \quad=\frac{\left(\cos ^2 \theta+\cos \theta+\sin ^2 \theta-2\right)+i(3 \sin \theta)}{4 \cos \theta+5}=\frac{\cos \theta-1}{4 \cos \theta+5}+i \frac{3 \sin \theta}{4 \cos \theta+5} \\
& \Rightarrow a=\frac{\cos \theta-1}{4 \cos \theta+5} ; b=\frac{3 \sin \theta}{4 \cos \theta+5}  \qquad ....(1)\\
& \Rightarrow 4 a \cos \theta+5 a=\cos \theta-1 \Rightarrow(4 a-1) \cos \theta=-(1+5 a) \text { or } \cos \theta=\frac{1+5 a}{1-4 a} \qquad ....(2)
\end{aligned}$

Also, $4 \mathrm{~b} \cos \theta+5 b=3 \sin \theta$.
$\text { i.e. } 3 \sin \theta=\frac{4 b(1+5 a)}{(1-4 a)}+5 b=\frac{4 b+20 a b+5 b-20 a b}{(1-4 a)}$
$\Rightarrow 3 \sin \theta=\frac{9 b}{1-4 a} \text { or } \sin \theta=\frac{3 b}{1-4 a}$ $\qquad ....(3)$
as, $\sin ^2 \theta+\cos ^2 \theta=1$
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{9 b^2}{(1-4 a)^2}+\frac{(1+5 a)^2}{(1-4 a)^2}=1 \\
& \text { i.e. }(1+5 a)^2+9 b^2=(1-4 a)^2 \ldots .(4) \\
& \text { (ii) from (1) } \frac{b}{a}=\frac{3 \sin \theta}{\cos \theta-1} \\
& \Rightarrow-\frac{b}{a}=\frac{6 \sin (\theta / 2) \cos (\theta / 2)}{2 \sin ^2(\theta / 2)}=3 \cot \left(\frac{\theta}{2}\right)
\end{aligned}$
(iii) from (4)
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow 25 a^2+10 a+1+9 b^2=16 a^2-8 a+1 \\
& \Rightarrow 9 a^2+18 a+9 b^2=0 \text { or } a^2+2 a+b^2=0
\end{aligned}$
$\text { i.e. } a^2+2 a+1+b^2=1$
$\Rightarrow(a+1)^2+b^2=1$
$\text { but }|z| \geq 0$
$\therefore|z|=1$

Hence, $|z|=1$
$\Rightarrow(a+1)^2+b^2=|z|$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Consider the two complex numbers $z$ and $w$ such that $w=\frac{Z-1}{Z+2}=a+b i$, where $a, b \in R$.Question: Which of the following is the value of $-\frac{b}{a}$, whenever it exists?
questions.solution:
(i) Consider $z=\operatorname{CiS} \theta$ and $a+i b=\frac{z-1}{z+2}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow a+i b=\frac{\operatorname{Cis} \theta-1}{\operatorname{Cis} \theta+2}=\frac{(\cos \theta-1)+i \sin \theta}{(\cos \theta+2)+i \sin \theta}=\frac{((\cos \theta-1)+i \sin \theta)((\cos \theta+2)-i \sin \theta)}{(\cos \theta+2)^2+\sin ^2 \theta} \\
& =\frac{\left((\cos \theta-1)(\cos \theta+2)+\sin ^2 \theta\right)+i\left((\cos \theta+2) \sin ^\theta \theta-(\cos \theta-1) \sin \theta\right)}{\cos ^2 \theta+4 \cos \theta+4+\sin ^2 \theta} \\
& \quad=\frac{\left(\cos ^2 \theta+\cos \theta+\sin ^2 \theta-2\right)+i(3 \sin \theta)}{4 \cos \theta+5}=\frac{\cos \theta-1}{4 \cos \theta+5}+i \frac{3 \sin \theta}{4 \cos \theta+5} \\
& \Rightarrow a=\frac{\cos \theta-1}{4 \cos \theta+5} ; b=\frac{3 \sin \theta}{4 \cos \theta+5}  \qquad ....(1)\\
& \Rightarrow 4 a \cos \theta+5 a=\cos \theta-1 \Rightarrow(4 a-1) \cos \theta=-(1+5 a) \text { or } \cos \theta=\frac{1+5 a}{1-4 a} \qquad ....(2)
\end{aligned}$

Also, $4 \mathrm{~b} \cos \theta+5 b=3 \sin \theta$.
$\text { i.e. } 3 \sin \theta=\frac{4 b(1+5 a)}{(1-4 a)}+5 b=\frac{4 b+20 a b+5 b-20 a b}{(1-4 a)}$
$\Rightarrow 3 \sin \theta=\frac{9 b}{1-4 a} \text { or } \sin \theta=\frac{3 b}{1-4 a}$ $\qquad ....(3)$
as, $\sin ^2 \theta+\cos ^2 \theta=1$
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{9 b^2}{(1-4 a)^2}+\frac{(1+5 a)^2}{(1-4 a)^2}=1 \\
& \text { i.e. }(1+5 a)^2+9 b^2=(1-4 a)^2 \ldots .(4) \\
& \text { (ii) from (1) } \frac{b}{a}=\frac{3 \sin \theta}{\cos \theta-1} \\
& \Rightarrow-\frac{b}{a}=\frac{6 \sin (\theta / 2) \cos (\theta / 2)}{2 \sin ^2(\theta / 2)}=3 \cot \left(\frac{\theta}{2}\right)
\end{aligned}$
(iii) from (4)
$\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow 25 a^2+10 a+1+9 b^2=16 a^2-8 a+1 \\
& \Rightarrow 9 a^2+18 a+9 b^2=0 \text { or } a^2+2 a+b^2=0
\end{aligned}$
$\text { i.e. } a^2+2 a+1+b^2=1$
$\Rightarrow(a+1)^2+b^2=1$
$\text { but }|z| \geq 0$
$\therefore|z|=1$

Hence, $|z|=1$
$\Rightarrow(a+1)^2+b^2=|z|$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
